creator: junovitch@freebsd.org
text:
Created attachment 163009
chromium-46.0.2490.80.patch

Should a user run /usr/local/bin/chrome from a GUI they may miss the message that a sysctl should be set.  I believe having that information in the pkg-message would be beneficial and helpful to new users.

As a response to https://forums.FreeBSD.org/threads/install-chrome-on-freebsd.53976/

Patch attached
www/chromium: add sysctl prerequisites to pkg-message

- Aid user who runs chrome from GUI vice CLI that may miss the sysctl requirement
- No PORTREVISION bump given trivial aspect of change
